text: China Factory Activity Worsens During Holiday in Official Survey - Bloomberg.com
fullText: China’s factory activity contracted more than forecast, according to official figures released on Wednesday, as the longest-ever Lunar New Year holiday brought a lull in manufacturing and construction.

text: Steve Toth declares victory in race to unseat U.S. Rep. Dan Crenshaw - The Texas Tribune
fullText: Steve Toth, one of the most conservative members of the Texas House, framed his challenge of Dan Crenshaw as a referendum on the ideological future of the Republican Party.

text: MLB Issues 162-Game PED Suspension To Jurickson Profar - MLB Trade Rumors
fullText: A second PED suspension will cost Braves outfielder Jurickson Profar the entire season. You can get all the details at MLB Trade Rumors.

text: CIA station in Saudi capital hit in drone attack - The Washington Post
fullText: The attack on the CIA station in Riyadh comes as Iran widens retaliation across the Middle East in the wake of the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign.

text: Blue Jays Re-Sign Joe Mantiply To Minor League Deal - MLB Trade Rumors
fullText: The Blue Jays bring back former All-Star reliever Joe Mantiply, who finished last season in their organization. MLB Trade Rumors has more.

text: Clock malfunctions cause 20-minute delay in Alabama-Georgia - On3
fullText: Clock malfunctions took over a lot of time in the second half in Athens, causing for a 20-minute delay in the Alabama-Georgia matchup.

text: Tensions flare as lawmakers question Iran war’s costs, risks and strategy - AP News
fullText: Questions are mounting at the U.S. Capitol over the Trump administration’s shifting rationale for going to war with Iran. Lawmakers are demanding answers over President Donald Trump's strategy, exit plan and costs to Americans in lives lost and dollars spent,…

text: Internal DHS watchdog: Noem is obstructing our work - Politico
fullText: The Office of the Inspector General said in a letter he has been denied access to records in at least 10 investigations.

text: Texas Supreme Court stops Dallas County from counting late votes after confusion over new rules - CNN
fullText: Changes to Election Day voting procedures in two Texas counties on Tuesday led to hundreds of voters being turned away from polling places, angry recriminations from candidates and an 11th hour scramble to delay poll closings.

text: Prehistoric water-dwelling weirdo with sideways teeth and a twisted jaw was already a 'living fossil' 275 million years ago - Live Science
fullText: Scientists have described Tanyka amnicola, a newly identified species of prehistoric creature that lived 275 million years ago and had a bizarre twisted jaw with sideways-facing teeth.

text: China is about to show the world its plan to win the future - CNN
fullText: China spent the last five years cultivating innovation and new technology at home. The next half decade will be dedicated to deploying the fruits of its labor to transform its economy – and its place in the world.

text: Mysterious 'dots' discovered by JWST may be the 1st stars in the universe on the verge of collapse - Live Science
fullText: A new study suggests that "little red dots" spied by the James Webb telescope could be the universe's short-lived first generation of gigantic stars, challenging an existing theory.

text: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra vs iPhone 17 Pro Max: Sample Photos Comparison - PhoneArena
fullText: The Galaxy S26 Ultra brings faster apertures and better image processing. Is that enough to beat the iPhone?

text: Palantir wants its old employees back: 'The shire is calling' - Business Insider
fullText: A Palantir exec is asking ex-employees to come back. In a post filled with Lord of the Rings references, he emphasized the intensity of the new roles.

text: ‘RJ Decker’ Review: Scott Speedman in ABC’s Breezy but Unconvincing Crime Procedural - The Hollywood Reporter
fullText: Adapted by Rob Doherty ('Elementary') from a novel by Carl Hiaasen, the series follows a Tampa photojournalist who reinvents himself as a private investigator after a stint in prison.

text: Movie Theater Chain iPic Files for Bankruptcy - Variety
fullText: iPic Theaters, a cinema chain that specializes in dine-in service, has filed for bankruptcy protection for a second time.

text: Adin Ross' Sister Madeline Dead at 36 - TMZ
fullText: Madeline Ross -- the sister of popular streamer Adin Ross -- has died.

text: Why Kon Knueppel deserves the NBA Rookie of the Year, but Cooper Flagg might get it anyway - SB Nation
fullText: The race for the ROY is hotter than it’s ever been.

text: Social media believed to be tied to Austin shooter is full of antisemitic, anti-Christian, misogynistic posts - CBS News
fullText: The attacker who opened fire at a bar in Austin, Texas, over the weekend appears to have posted antisemitic, anti-Christian and misogynistic messages on social media.

text: A Possible US Government iPhone-Hacking Toolkit Is Now in the Hands of Foreign Spies and Criminals - WIRED
fullText: A highly sophisticated set of iPhone hijacking techniques has likely infected tens of thousands of phones or more. Clues suggest it was originally built for the US government.
